audit_result.proof_trace: Metadata refresh uses fixed 30-minute timer (MetadataRequestFrequency, line 2208) with no fork-aware acceleration. At Fulu activation, existing peers retain custody_group_count=0 for up to 30 minutes because peerPingerHeartbeat (line 2211) checks only elapsed time, not fork transitions.
audit_result.attack_scenario: All peers connected before Fulu fork retain stale custody_group_count=0 for up to 30 minutes. matchIntersection filter (line 374-384) computes zero overlap with remoteCustodyGroupCount=0, blocking data column sync network-wide during the critical fork transition window.

PROP-6a4369e9-inv-049.text: After a protocol upgrade or fork transition, all peer metadata fields that are semantically redefined by the new protocol version (e.g., custody_group_count becoming meaningful at Fulu) must be refreshed from each connected peer within a bounded time. Stale pre-transition default values (e.g., zero) must not be used for post-transition decisions.
PROP-6a4369e9-inv-049.assertion: forall peer, field in fork_redefined_metadata: time_since_fork_activation() > REFRESH_BOUND => peer.metadata[field] reflects post-fork value, not pre-fork default
